questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The larger of two angles made with the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>-axis of a straight line drawn through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo></math> so that it intersects the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> at a point distant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></math> from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
Let A=(1,2) equation of line is

x-1cosθ=y-2sinθ=r=63

∴ x=63cosθ+1,y=63sinθ+2

This point lies on line x+y=4

∴ 63cosθ+63sinθ=1

⇒ sinθ+cosθ=32

⇒ sinπ4+θ=32

⇒ π4+θ=60° or 120°

⇒ θ=15° or 75°

∴ Larger angle =75°
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $(-4,5)$ is one vertex and $7 x-y+8=0$ is one diagonal of a square, then the equation of second diagonal is
questions.solution:
One vertex of square is $(-4,5)$ and equation of one diagonal is $7 x-y+8=0$ Diagonal of a square are perpendicular and bisect each other
Let the equation of the other diagonal be $y=m x+$ $c$ where $m$ is the slope of the line and $c$ is the $\mathrm{y}$-intercept.
Since this line passes through $(-4,5)$


Since this line is at right angle to the line
$7 x-y+8=0$ or $y=7 x+8$, having slope $=7$,
$\therefore 7 \times \mathrm{m}=-1$ or $\mathrm{m}=\frac{-1}{7}$
Putting this value of $m$ in equation (i) we get
$5=-4 \times\left(\frac{-1}{7}\right)+c$
or $5=\frac{4}{7}+c \quad$ or $\quad c=5-\frac{4}{7}=\frac{31}{7}$
Hence equation of the other diagonal is
$y=-\frac{1}{7} x+\frac{31}{7}$ or $7 y=-x+31$ or $x+7 y-31=0$ or $x+7 y=31$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the lines $4 x+3 y-1=0, x-y+5=0$ and $k x+5 y-3=0$ are concurrent, then $k$ is equal to :
questions.solution:
Since, the given three lines are concurrent, then
$\left|\begin{array}{rrr}4 & 3 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 5 \\ k & 5 & -3\end{array}\right|=0$
$\Rightarrow 4(3-25)-3(-3-5 k)-1(5+k)=0$
$\Rightarrow-88+9+15 k-5-k=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad 14 k=84$
$\therefore \quad k=6$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The distance of the point \((-1,1)\) from the line \(12(x+6)=5(y-2)\) is
questions.solution:
The given line is \(12(x+6)=5(y-2)\)
\(\Rightarrow 12 x+72=5 y-10\)
or \(12 x-5 y+72+10=0 \Rightarrow 12 x-5 y+82=0\) The perpendicular distance from \(\left(x_1, y_1\right)\) to the line \(a x+b y+c=0\) is \(\frac{\left(\mathrm{ax}_1+\mathrm{by}_1+\mathrm{c}\right)}{\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2}}\). The point \(\left(x_1, y_1\right)\) is \((-1,1)\), therefore, perpendicular distance from \((-1,1)\) to the line \(12 x-5 y+82=0\) is
\(=\frac{|-12-5+82|}{\sqrt{12^2+(-5)^2}}=\frac{65}{\sqrt{144+25}}=\frac{65}{\sqrt{169}}=5\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A rectangle \(A B C D\) has its side parallel to the line \(y=2 x\) and vertices \(A, B, D\) are on lines \(y=1, x=1\) and \(x=-1\) respectively. The coordinate of \(C\) can be
questions.solution:
Hint: Case I
line \(y=2 x\ldots(1)\) \(A D\) parallel to \(y=2 x\) \(\Rightarrow \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{AD}}=2 ~\&~ \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{AB}}=-\frac{1}{2}\) for \(a=3\) \(A(3,1), B(1,2), C(-3,-6), D(-1,-7)\)
\(\begin{aligned} & \text { for } a=-3 \\ & C(3,3), A(-3,1), B(1,-1), D(-1,5)\end{aligned}\)
Case II AD perpendicular to line (1) \(\Rightarrow M_{A D}=\frac{1}{2}, M_{A B}=2 \Rightarrow b=3-2 a \Rightarrow d=\frac{a+1}{2}\)
for \(a=3, C(-3,-2)\)
for \(a=-3, C(3,7)\)
\({ }^*\) In both cases if abscissa of \(C: 3\) or -3 then co-ordinates of \(C\) can be \((3,3)\) or \((3,7)\) or \((-3,-6)\) or \((-3,-2)\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the distance between the points $(a \cos \theta, a \sin \theta)$ and $(a \cos \phi, a \sin \phi)$ is $2 a$, then $\theta$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let the points be $A=(a \cos \theta, a \sin \theta)$ and
$$
\begin{aligned}
& B=(a \cos \phi, a \sin \phi) \\
& \therefore \quad A B=\sqrt{(a \cos \theta-a \cos \phi)^2}+(a \sin \theta-a \sin \phi)^2 \\
& =\sqrt{a^2 \cos ^2 \theta+a^2 \cos ^2 \phi-2 a^2 \cos \theta \cos \phi} \\
& \quad \quad+a^2 \sin ^2 \theta+a^2 \sin ^2 \phi-2 a^2 \sin \theta \sin \phi
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\sqrt{2 a^2-2 a^2(\cos \theta \cos \phi+\sin \theta \sin \phi)} \\
& =\sqrt{2} a(\sqrt{1-\cos (\theta-\phi)} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 a=\sqrt{2} a \sqrt{2} \sin \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \sin \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right)=1 \Rightarrow \frac{\theta-\phi}{2}=n \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \sin \left(\frac{\theta-\phi}{2}\right)=1 \Rightarrow \frac{\theta-\phi}{2}=n \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \theta-\phi=2 n \pi \pm \pi \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \theta=2 n \pi \pm \pi+\phi \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
where, $n \in Z$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of a given straight line is $\frac{x-x_1}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y-y_1}{\sin \theta}=\gamma$. If the equation of the line perpendicular to the given line and passing through $(\alpha, \beta)$ is $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=1$, then $\frac{b}{a}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given equation of line is $\frac{x-x_1}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y-y_1}{\sin \theta}=\gamma$ Slope of Eq. (i) is $m=\tan \theta$
So, slope of a line perpendicular to Eq. (i) is $(-\cot \theta)$. According to the question, equation of required line is $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=1$ Slope of line (ii) is $\left(\frac{-b}{a}\right)$ So, $\frac{-b}{a}=-\cot \theta \Rightarrow \frac{b}{a}=\cot \theta$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A straight line passing through a fixed point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> intersects the coordinate axes at two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If the locus of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> which forms a rectangle with the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>O</mi></math> (origin) and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>h</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>h</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
A Straight line passing through a fixed point 3,5 and intersects coordinate axis at point A and B.

Let coordinates of Ap,0 and B0,q

∴ Intercept form of line is xp+yq=1

Here, point 3,5 will satisfy the line.

⇒ 3p+5q=1

Here, coordinate of point are C(x,y)=(p,q)

Hence, to find locus of C substitute p=x & q=y in the above equation.

⇒ 3x+5y=1 

⇒ 5x+3y-xy=0

Now compare ax+by+2hxy=0 to 5x+3y-xy=0

a=5  b=3,  h=-12  

a+b+h ⇒ 5+3-12=152.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of possible straight lines passing through the point $(2,3)$, while forming a triangle with coordinate axes enclosing an area 12 sq. units is
questions.solution:
Let the equation of line forming a triangle having area $12 \mathrm{sq}$. units is
$$
\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=1
$$

So,
$$
|a b|=24
$$
$\because$ Line (i) passes through point $(2,3)$, so
$$
\frac{2}{a}+\frac{3}{b}=1
$$
from Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get
$$
|2 b+3 a|=24
$$

If $a$ and $b$ are positive, then
$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& & a b=24 \text { and } 3 a+2 b=24 \\
\Rightarrow & a\left(\frac{24-3 a}{2}\right)=24 \\
\Rightarrow & 3 a^2-24 a+48=0 \\
& \because \text { Discriminant }=24^2-4 \times 3 \times 48=0 \\
\therefore & a=4 \text { and } b=6
\end{array}
$$

If $a$ is positive and $b$ is negative then $a b=-24$ and $3 a+2 b=24$.
$$
\Rightarrow \quad 2\left(\frac{24-3 a}{2}\right)=-24 \Rightarrow 3 a^2-24 a-48=0
$$
$\because$ Discriminant $=24^2+4 \times 3 \times 48>0$


$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore \quad a=\frac{24 \pm \sqrt{2 \times 24^2}}{6}=4+4 \sqrt{2} \quad \because a>0 \\
\text { and } \quad b=6-6 \sqrt{2}
\end{array}
$$

If $a$ is negative and $b$ is positive, then $a b=-24$ and $3 a+2 b=24$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad a=4-4 \sqrt{2} \text { and } b=6+6 \sqrt{2}
$$

And at the last it is not possible that $a$ and $b$ both are negative.
So, 3 triangles are possible.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is a point inside the <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math>. Three straight lines parallel to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> and <math><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi></math> are drawn which divides the triangle into six regions. If the area of three triangles formed in these regions are <math><mn>9,16</mn></math> and <math><mn>25</mn></math> square units, then the area (in sq. units) of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
∵ΔABC, ΔTUP, ΔPDE, ΔSPR all are similar
∴ ratio of areas =ratio of sides2
Let, PT=α=AS
∴ SRPT=45⇒SR=4α5
PEPT=35⇒PE=3α5
⇒AC=AS+SR+RC
=α+4α5+3α5
=12α5
∴arΔABCarΔPDE=AC2PE2=122·α252·32α252
Hence, arΔABC=16arΔPDE=144 sq. units
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Equation of the locus of the centroid of the triangle whose vertices are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>a</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>k</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>b</mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>k</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> where <math><mi>k</mi></math> is a parameter, is
questions.solution:
Let, the centroid be x, y, then

x=acosk+bsink+13 & y=asink-bcosk+03

⇒ 3x-1=acosk+bsink  ...i

⇒ 3y=asink-bcosk  ...ii

Square i & ii then add  we get,  

⇒3x-12+3y2=a2+b2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the line passing through the point of intersection of the lines $x-3 y+2=0$ and $2 x+5 y-7=0$ and perpendicular to the line $3 x+2 y+5=0$ is :
questions.solution:
The equation of lines are 
$x-3 y+2=0$ ...(i)
and $2 x+5 y-7=0$ ...(ii)
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$x=1, y=1$
$\therefore$ The co-ordinates of a point of intersection of given lines are $(1,1)$.
The equation of line perpendicular to $3 x+2 y+5=0$ is $2 x-3 y+\lambda=0$.
Which passes through $(1,1)$.
$\therefore \quad 2-3+\lambda=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \lambda=1$
$\therefore$ Required equation of line is
$2 x-3 y+1=0$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the sides of a triangle are given by the equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the coordinates of its orthocentre are
questions.solution:
As two lines are perpendicular, so triangle is right angle Δ where right angle is made at point of intersection of these two lines.
Also in right angle triangle, orthocentre is point of intersection of perpendicular lines.
⇒ orthocentre is point of intersection of lines x-y+1=0 and x+y+3=0 i.e. -2,-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A point $P$ on a line is at a distance of 4 units from the origin $(0,0)$. If the line makes $60^{\circ}$ with the negative direction of the $X$-axis, then $P$ is
questions.solution:
$O P$ is perpendicular to given line.
$\therefore$ Slope of $O P=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{y}{x} \Rightarrow x=\sqrt{3} y$ Also $x^2+y^2=16$ $\therefore 3 y^2+y^2=16 \Rightarrow y= \pm 2$ $\therefore$ Points can be $(2 \sqrt{3}, 2)$ or $(-2 \sqrt{3},-2)$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Point <math><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1,7</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> lies on the line <math><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>17</mn><mo>.</mo></math> Then the coordinates of the points farthest from the line which are at a distance of <math><mn>10</mn></math> units from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> are
questions.solution:
There are two points A & B which are at a distance of 10 units from P and farthest ( 10 unit distance) from the line 4x+3y=17.

The slope of AB=34 {Because the slope of 4x+3y=17 is -43 }
Let line AB makes an angle θ with x-axis then tan⁡θ=34⇒cos⁡θ,sin⁡θ=45,35
⇒A or B=10cos⁡θ-1,10sin⁡θ+7 or ⇒A or B=-10cos⁡θ-1,-10sin⁡θ+7
⇒A or B=7,13 or -9,1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the centroid and circumcenter of a triangle are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, respectively, then the orthocentre of the triangle is _____.
questions.solution:
The orthocenter, centroid and circumcenter of the triangle are collinear and lie on the line called Euler Line.

Also the centroid divides the line joining orthocenter and circumcenter in the ratio 2 : 1.

So centroid 3,3 would be dividing x,y and 6,2 in the ratio of 2 : 1.

So, 12+x3=3, therefore x=9-12= -3, and 4+y3=3, therefore y=5.

Hence, -3, 5 is the answer.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The area of a triangle is <math> <mrow> <mn>5.</mn> </mrow> </math> If two of its vertices are <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and the third vertex lies on the line <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></math>, then the third vertex is -
questions.solution:
Let the third vertex be h, k

Since it lies on line y=x+3

∴ k=h+3 ........(1)

Also area of triangle is 5

∴122-2-k+3k-1+h 1+2=± 5

⇒k+3h- 7=± 10 ........(2)

Solving (1) & (2) we get

h=72, k =132⇒ 72,132

and h=-32, k=32⇒ -32,32
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A straight line $L$ through the point $(3,-2)$ is inclined at an angle $60^{\circ}$ to the line $\sqrt{3} x+y=1$. If $L$ also intersects the $X$-axis, then the equation of $L$ is
questions.solution:
A straight line passing through $P$ and making an angle of $\alpha=60^{\circ}$, is given by $\frac{y-y_1}{x-x_1}=\tan (\theta \pm \alpha)$
where, $\quad \sqrt{3} x+y=1$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad y=-\sqrt{3} x+1 $$ Then, $\quad \tan \theta=-\sqrt{3}$ $$ \begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & \frac{y+2}{x-3}=\frac{\tan \theta \pm \tan \alpha}{1 \mp \tan \theta \tan \alpha} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{y+2}{x-3}=\frac{-\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{3}}{1-(-\sqrt{3})(\sqrt{3})} \\ \text { and } & \frac{y+2}{x-3}=\frac{-\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{3}}{1+(-\sqrt{3})(\sqrt{3})} \end{array} $$ $$ \begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & y+2=0 \\ \text { and } & \frac{y+2}{x-3}=\frac{-2 \sqrt{3}}{1-3}=\sqrt{3} \\ \Rightarrow & y+2=\sqrt{3} x-3 \sqrt{3} \end{array} $$ Neglecting, $y+2=0$ as it does not intersect $Y$-axis.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are collinear, then the number of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Let A≡-2,0, B=-1,13, C≡cosθ,sinθ

Equation to AB,

y-0=13-0-1+2x+2

⇒3y=x+2, Point C lies on this line.

⇒3sinθ-cosθ=2

⇒32sinθ-12cosθ=1

⇒sinθ-π6=1, where θ∈0,2π

θ-π6=π2

⇒θ=π2+π6=2π3

So, only one value of θ is possible.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
